segments.text: Do you know this girl is 60 years old and it beats milliondoll marketing campaigns every single day. When this Amul girl was created in 1966, she was
just a simple handdrawn doodle to sell butter. But while other brands spend millions constantly redesigning their logos, Amul has not changed once. So how
did she survive that long? See, cute mascots usually [music] get boring very fast. To survive for decades, you need more than just a cute design. The genius [music] design, they gave her a voice.
Instead of just selling butter, they used her to comment on everything from roasting politicians to celebrating cricket wins and Bollywood [music]
gossip. But how does a butter brand talk about everything controversial without getting cancelled? Because she says exactly what the common man is already thinking. She's a mirror of India. So
what's the most iconic Amulad you remember? Tell me in the comments.
